title: IssuerRegistry Smart Contract: DID-to-Issuer Trust Mapping On-Chain
description: Implement the IssuerRegistry Solidity smart contract. This contract stores a mapping from DID strings to their trust status and metadata. It allows a governance admin (owner) to register, update, and revoke trusted issuers. Sentinel nodes query this contract to determine whether a credential's issuer is trusted before accepting VCs.
objectives: On-chain authoritative list of trusted VC issuers
Events emitted for all state changes — used by Python event indexer (TASK-020)
Read-only functions do not modify state — usable by any web3 client

steps: Step 1 - Contract Declaration: pragma solidity ^0.8.25;. Inherits: AccessControl (OpenZeppelin v5), Pausable (OpenZeppelin v5). Define bytes32 constant ISSUER_ADMIN_ROLE = keccak256('ISSUER_ADMIN_ROLE');

Step 2 - IssuerRecord Struct: struct IssuerRecord { string did; string name; string description; uint256 registeredAt; uint256 updatedAt; bool active; string metadataURI; }. metadataURI is an optional IPFS CID or HTTPS URL with extended issuer metadata — NOT used for trust decisions, informational only.

Step 3 - Storage: mapping(bytes32 => IssuerRecord) private _issuers; where the key is keccak256(abi.encodePacked(did)). Array of issuer DID hashes for enumeration: bytes32[] private _issuerKeys. mapping(bytes32 => uint256) private _issuerKeyIndex; (for O(1) deletion from array).

Step 4 - Events: event IssuerRegistered(bytes32 indexed didHash, string did, string name, address indexed registeredBy); event IssuerRevoked(bytes32 indexed didHash, string did, address indexed revokedBy); event IssuerUpdated(bytes32 indexed didHash, string did, address indexed updatedBy);

Step 5 - registerIssuer(string calldata did, string calldata name, string calldata description, string calldata metadataURI): onlyRole(ISSUER_ADMIN_ROLE). Validate: did length > 0 and <= 2048. Compute didHash. Require: !_issuers[didHash].active (duplicate check). Set IssuerRecord with block.timestamp. Push to _issuerKeys. Emit IssuerRegistered.

Step 6 - revokeIssuer(string calldata did): onlyRole(ISSUER_ADMIN_ROLE). Compute didHash. Require: _issuers[didHash].active. Set active=false, updatedAt=block.timestamp. Swap-and-pop from _issuerKeys array. Emit IssuerRevoked.

Step 7 - updateIssuer(string calldata did, string calldata name, string calldata description, string calldata metadataURI): onlyRole(ISSUER_ADMIN_ROLE). Require issuer exists and is active. Update fields (cannot change did). Set updatedAt=block.timestamp. Emit IssuerUpdated.

Step 8 - Read Functions: isIssuerActive(string calldata did) returns (bool) — pure view, no auth required. getIssuer(string calldata did) returns (IssuerRecord memory) — view, no auth. getIssuerCount() returns (uint256). getIssuerAtIndex(uint256 index) returns (IssuerRecord memory) — for enumeration by Python indexer.

Step 9 - IIssuerRegistry Interface: Define in contracts/interfaces/IIssuerRegistry.sol. Export all external functions. Python client and other contracts always interact via the interface, not the concrete type.
Step 10 - Constructor: constructor(address initialAdmin). grantRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE, initialAdmin). grantRole(ISSUER_ADMIN_ROLE, initialAdmin). DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE allows managing role assignments.

Step 11 - pauseRegistry() / unpauseRegistry(): onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE). whenNotPaused modifier on registerIssuer and updateIssuer. revokeIssuer should work even when paused (emergency revocation).

IIssuerRegistry.isIssuerActive: function isIssuerActive(string calldata did) external view returns (bool)
IIssuerRegistry.getIssuer: function getIssuer(string calldata did) external view returns (IssuerRecord memory)
IIssuerRegistry.registerIssuer: function registerIssuer(string calldata did, string calldata name, string calldata description, string calldata metadataURI) external

security_constraints: Use OpenZeppelin AccessControl — do not implement custom role system
DID string length validated on-chain: max 2048 bytes to prevent gas griefing
Swap-and-pop pattern for array deletion to prevent O(n) gas costs
No msg.value — this contract is not payable, add nonpayable checks
Emit events for ALL state changes — required for off-chain indexing
Constructor sets DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE to initialAdmin — do not hardcode any address

technical_requirements: Gas optimization: use bytes32 keys in mappings, not strings
acceptance_criteria: Compiles without warnings with solidity 0.8.25 optimizer 200 runs
revokeIssuer emits IssuerRevoked and sets active=false
Duplicate DID registration reverts with IssuerAlreadyRegistered
All test cases pass in npx hardhat test
